_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to write legibly, fluently and automatically — so that handwriting does not slow down our thinking.

_source.w: We are learning to write legibly, fluently and automatically — so that handwriting does not slow down our thinking.
y: Automatic handwriting frees our brain to focus on the content and quality of our writing.
Fluent, legible handwriting is a practical skill that serves us in tests, note-taking and daily work.
At this level, handwriting should support rather than hinder our communication.
eg: We write for sustained periods without needing to stop and think about letter formation.
We maintain consistent, legible handwriting even when writing quickly.
We adapt our handwriting for different purposes — neat for final copies, quick for notes.
We practise writing at speed while maintaining readability.
We reflect on our handwriting habits and identify areas for improvement.
We use joined handwriting fluently across all our school work.
